command: dism /Mount-Image /ImageFile:C:\images\install_clean.wim /Index:1 /MountDir:C:\Mount\Base; dism /Mount-Image /ImageFile:C:\images\install_mod.wim /Index:1 /MountDir:C:\Mount\Win; New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path C:\Mount\Win\Windows\System32\Tasks | Out-Null; Copy-Item C:\staging\MyTask.xml C:\Mount\Win\Windows\System32\Tasks\MyTask.xml -Force; Compare-Object (Get-ChildItem -Name C:\Mount\Base\Windows\System32\Tasks -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) (Get-ChildItem -Name C:\Mount\Win\Windows\System32\Tasks -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)
explanation: Adds a scheduled task XML to the offline image and uses Compare-Object to detect the new task file compared to the baseline.
rationale_steps: Mount base and modified images via DISM.
Copy a task XML into the modified image.
List and compare task directory contents using Compare-Object.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Valid PowerShell with semicolon-separated commands, balanced parentheses, and correct DISM/cmdlet parameter usage.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It mounts WIMs and adds a file to the mounted image then compares directories, but lacks commit/unmount to persist changes and requires admin privileges, so the modification to the system image is incomplete despite aligning with T1601.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is well-formed with balanced parentheses, proper semicolon separators, valid DISM flags, and correct PowerShell cmdlet syntax throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command correctly mounts a clean and modified WIM image offline, injects a scheduled task XML into the modified image's task directory, and uses Compare-Object to diff the two mounts—accurately demonstrating offline image tampering consistent with T1601, though the 'None' privilege claim is unrealistic since DISM mount operations require administrator privileges.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command sequence uses proper PowerShell syntax, valid DISM flags, and correct command separators with balanced parameters.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command successfully modifies the mounted image and verifies the change, though it lacks the final DISM commit flag to save changes back to the WIM file and requires elevated privileges not reflected in the prompt.
